ddls:
CREATE TABLE "clinical_variables" (
  "variable_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each clinical variable */,
  "variable_name" TEXT /* Name of the clinical variable */,
  "variable_description" TEXT /* Description of the clinical variable */,
  "variable_type" TEXT /* Type of the clinical variable (e.g., continuous, categorical) */,
  "unit_of_measurement" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the variable (e.g., mg/dL, kg) */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data (e.g., survey, medical records) */,
  "data_collection_method" TEXT /* Method used to collect the data (e.g., self-reported, lab test) */,
  "documentation_link" TEXT /* Link to detailed documentation for the variable */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("variable_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "relationships" (
  "relationship_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship */,
  "from_variable_id" INTEGER /* ID of the clinical variable from which the relationship is measured */,
  "to_variable_id" INTEGER /* ID of the clinical variable to which the relationship is measured (likely SES.CD.Score in this context) */,
  "direction" TEXT /* Direction of the relationship (e.g., positive, negative) */,
  "weight" INTEGER /* Weight of the relationship */,
  "sig" INTEGER /* Significance level of the relationship */,
  "correlation_type" TEXT /* Type of correlation (e.g., Pearson, Spearman) */,
  "p_value" REAL /* P-value for the relationship */,
  "confidence_interval_lower" REAL /* Lower bound of the confidence interval */,
  "confidence_interval_upper" REAL /* Upper bound of the confidence interval */,
  "study_id" TEXT /* ID of the study in which the relationship was observed */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the relationship */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relationship_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_relationships_from_variable_id FOREIGN KEY ("from_variable_id") REFERENCES clinical_variables ("variable_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_relationships_to_variable_id FOREIGN KEY ("to_variable_id") REFERENCES clinical_variables ("variable_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "patients" (
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each patient */,
  "patient_name" TEXT /* Name of the patient */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the patient */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the patient */,
  "SES_score" REAL /* Socioeconomic status score of the patient */,
  "address" TEXT /* Patient's address */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Patient's phone number */,
  "email" TEXT /* Patient's email */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Patient's occupation */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Patient's education level */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* Patient's marital status */,
  "smoking_status" TEXT /* Patient's smoking status (e.g., smoker, non-smoker) */,
  "alcohol_consumption" TEXT /* Patient's alcohol consumption */,
  "medical_history" TEXT /* Patient's medical history */,
  "family_history" TEXT /* Patient's family medical history */,
  "consent_form_signed" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the patient has signed the consent form */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "clinical_measurements" (
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measurement */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient for whom the measurement was taken */,
  "variable_id" INTEGER /* ID of the clinical variable measured */,
  "measurement_value" REAL /* Value of the measurement */,
  "measurement_date" TEXT /* Date when the measurement was taken */,
  "measurement_location" TEXT /* Location where the measurement was taken (e.g., hospital, home) */,
  "measurement_method" TEXT /* Method used to take the measurement (e.g., blood test, questionnaire) */,
  "measurement_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the measurement */,
  "measurement_source" TEXT /* Source of the measurement (e.g., primary care, specialist) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_clinical_measurements_variable_id FOREIGN KEY ("variable_id") REFERENCES clinical_variables ("variable_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_clinical_measurements_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dietary_intake" (
  "intake_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dietary intake record */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient for whom the intake was recorded */,
  "food_item" TEXT /* Name of the food item */,
  "amount" REAL /* Amount of the food item consumed */,
  "intake_date" TEXT /* Date when the food item was consumed */,
  "meal_type" TEXT /* Type of meal (e.g., breakfast, lunch, dinner) */,
  "meal_time" TEXT /* Time of the meal */,
  "food_group" TEXT /* Food group of the item (e.g., fruits, vegetables) */,
  "nutritional_value" TEXT /* Nutritional value of the food item */,
  "intake_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the intake */,
  "intake_source" TEXT /* Source of the intake data (e.g., self-reported, food diary) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("intake_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dietary_intake_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user account is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was last updated */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON object containing user permissions */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
